Family History Record · Ireland
Family
History
O'Keeffe · O'Connor · Fitzgerald · O'Leary · Curtin · Kissane · Scanlan · Brosnan
This record documents the family history of David Gerard (Ger) O'Keeffe and his wife Catriona (Katie) Curtin, tracing eight grandparent lines across Counties Cork, Kerry and Limerick, Ireland. The record spans from the mid-18th century — with the O'Leary line confirmed to 1746 — to the present generation. All facts are drawn from primary sources: civil birth, marriage and death certificates, Irish census returns of 1901, 1911 and 1926, gravestone inscriptions, military pension archives, Ancestry family tree records, and oral history.
Co. Cork
Ballydesmond
Shanavoher
Lake Vale
Glenalocha
Knocknagree
Kiskeam
Co. Kerry
Scartaglin
Mount
Castleisland
Gneeveguilla
Rathmore
Lisselton
Co. Limerick
Tournafulla
Killaculleen
Glenquin South
Barna
Monagea
Brosna
This document is intended to be read by all family members across generations. It is a living record — to be added to as new sources are found and new generations are born.
Version 14 · Compiled 1 May 2026
Contents
This document is organised by family name. Each section covers one family branch — its origins, key people, timeline of events, and verified records. Sections 1–4 trace the family of Gerard (Ger) O'Keeffe across County Cork and Kerry. Sections 5–6 trace the family of his wife Catriona (Katie) Curtin across County Limerick and Kerry. The Appendix explains how all six families connect.
Section 1
The O'Keeffe Family
  • Ballyduane, Shanavoher & Glenalocha, Co. Cork · 1820s to 1960s
  • Origins: John O'Keeffe & Ellen Connors, Newmarket, Co. Cork
  • William O'Keeffe — two marriages to the O'Connor sisters

Section 2
The Fitzgerald & Reen Family
  • Gullane, Gneeveguilla & Mount Infant, Knocknagree, Co. Cork · 1820s to 1967

  • Thomas E. Fitzgerald & Ellen Reen — Helena's parents
  • The Runaway Bog — how the family came to Mount Infant
  • Helena Fitzgerald — marriage to Eugene O'Keeffe, 1925 · wedding photograph · certificate No. 114

Section 3
The O'Connor Family
  • Dromulton, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry · 1850s to 1970

  • The tragedy of Daniel O'Connor · Edmond O'Connor & Bridget Brosnan

  • Wedding photograph · Marriage certificate No. 402, Castleisland, 1925

Section 4
The O'Leary Family
  • Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry · 1746 to 1982
  • Deep origins: William O'Leary (b.1746) · Michael O'Leary · Griffith's Valuation 1853 · Rent book 1852–1876
  • Jeremiah O'Leary & Ellen Kerins — 11 children at Mount Scartaglin
  • James O'Leary & Hannah Scanlan — 87-acre farm · Cornelius O'Leary, NYPD Commissioner
  • Family photograph c.1930 · Commandant Jer O'Leary — killed in custody 1923
  • James O'Leary's fifteen-year pension struggle · eyewitness account
  • Timeline · Census records 1901 & 1926 · Scanlon family census records, Brosna 1901 & 1911
Section 5
The Curtin Family
  • Killaculleen & Tournafulla, Co. Limerick · c.1820 to present
  • Origins: Laurence John Curtin (b.c.1820) & Johanna O'Brien, Barna, Co. Limerick
  • Maurice Laurence Curtin — two marriages · Killaculleen farm
  • Daniel Curtin & Bridget (Babe) Kissane — parents of Noreen Curtin
  • Noreen Curtin — marriage to Michael Curtin, 1961 · parents of Catriona (Katie) Curtin
  • Timeline · Census record 1926 · Marriage certificates · Children of Daniel & Bridget
Section 6
The Curtin Family
  • Meenahela & Glenquin South, Tournafulla, Co. Limerick · 1772 to present
  • Deep origins: Michael Curtin (1772–1834) · Ballycommane · William P. Curtin (1841–1922) · Meenahela gravestone
  • William P. Curtin & Julia McCarthy — eight children · Meenahela, Tournafulla
  • William W. Curtin & Mary O'Sullivan — Creamery Manager · 88-acre farm · Glenquin South
  • Michael Curtin — marriage to Noreen Curtin, 1961 · father of Catriona (Katie) Curtin

Section 7
Living Generations

Appendix
How the Families Connect
  • How Sections 1–6 come together in Gerard O'Keeffe & Catriona (Katie) Curtin
Section 1
The O'Keeffe Family
Ballyduane, Shanavoher & Glenalocha, Co. Cork · 1820s to 1960s
Generation Tree · Section 1
Origins — John O'Keeffe & Ellen Connors / Owen O'Connor & Julia Dennehy
John O'Keeffe & Ellen Connors
John b. 1827, d. 1906 · Married 21 February 1852, Newmarket, Co. Cork
William's Parents — 10 Children
Ellen (1852), William (1854), Daniel (1855), Mary (1856), John (1858), Bridget (1860), Cornelius (1862), Laurence (1865), Catherine (1868), James (1869), Hugh (1871). John O'Keeffe witnessed his son William's 1883 marriage.
Owen O'Connor & Julia Dennehy
Julia Dennehy b. c.1827 · Glenalocha, Co. Cork
Julia & Hannah's Parents — 3 Known Children
Julia O'Connor (b.1863), Mary O'Connor (b.23 Jul 1868, married Peter Riordan), Johanna/Hannah O'Connor (b.26 Jul 1871). Owen witnessed daughter Julia's 1883 marriage. Both his daughters Julia and Hannah would marry the same man — William O'Keeffe.
The Riordan Family — First Cousins through Mary O'Connor

Mary O'Connor & Peter Riordan — Mary O'Connor (b.23 July 1868, sister of Julia & Hannah) married Peter Riordan (b.1857) at Kingwilliamstown RC, 21 July 1886. Mary died 24 June 1899 aged 30, of pneumonia, leaving seven children motherless. Peter died 28 February 1927.

Children — All First Cousins of the O'Keeffe Children at Shanavoher
NameBornNotes
Ellen Riordan4 Aug 1887—
Julia Riordan27 Apr 1889—
Hannah Riordan27 Jan 1891Titanic survivor — see below
Mary Riordan17 May 1892, d. 24 Jun 1899Died same day as her mother
Hanora Mary Riordan18 May 1894—
James Riordan27 Mar 1896—
Eugene Riordan3 Dec 1897—
Hannah Riordan — RMS Titanic Survivor, 1912

Hannah Riordan was a first cousin of the O'Keeffe children at Shanavoher — her mother was Mary O'Connor, sister of Julia and Hannah O'Connor. She boarded the Titanic at Queenstown on 11 April 1912 as a 3rd class passenger (ticket 334915, £7 14s 5d), travelling to join her sister Ellen in New York. She was rescued in Lifeboat 13. Her cousin Patrick O'Connor and friend John Meehan were among those lost.

She was naturalised as a US citizen on 10 May 1928 — described as a maid, 5'9", brown hair, grey eyes, living at 109 East 72nd Street, New York. She married John "Jack" Spollen (b.11 Sep 1899, Co. Offaly) on St Patrick's Day, 17 March 1936 at St Anselm's Church, the Bronx. No children. She died 29 September 1982 aged 91 in the Bronx and is buried at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Hawthorne, New York. Her husband John died 4 April 1983. Hannah rarely spoke about the Titanic and refused all interviews.
William O'Keeffe — Two Marriages to the O'Connor Sisters
William O'Keeffe
Born March 1854, Ballyduane, Newmarket, Co. Cork · Died 27 April 1901, Glenalougha, aged 47
Father & Mother
John O'Keeffe (b.1827, d.1906), farmer, Ballyduane & Ellen Connors (married 21 Feb 1852, Newmarket)
Cause of Death
Mitral Valve Disease, 3 months certified. Died 67 days after his second marriage. Hannah was present.
Julia O'Connor — 1st Wife
Born 8 Nov 1863, Glenalocha · Died 4 July 1898, Shanavoher, aged 35
Married 3 April 1883, Kingwilliamstown. Six children 1885–1894.
Cause of Death Anarca Causa, 3 years certified. Left six children aged 4–13.
Johanna (Hannah) O'Connor — 2nd Wife
Born 26 July 1871, Glenalocha · Died 22 May 1940, Lakevale
Married 19 Feb 1901. Julia's younger sister. Widowed 67 days later. Raised Julia's children plus her own daughter Ellen for ~40 years as a widow and head of household.
Chronological Timeline — Part 1
  • 21 Feb 1852
    John O'Keeffe marries Ellen ConnorsNewmarket, Co. Cork. William's parents. John (b.1827) was a farmer at Ballyduane; they would have ten children.
  • 8 Nov 1863
    Julia O'Connor bornGlenalocha, Co. Cork. Daughter of Owen O'Connor & Julia Dennehy, farmers.
  • 23 Jul 1868
    Mary O'Connor bornGlenalocha. Later married Peter Riordan. Their daughter Hannah would survive the Titanic.
  • 26 Jul 1871
    Johanna (Hannah) O'Connor bornGlenalocha. Younger sister of Julia and Mary. She would marry William O'Keeffe after Julia's death.
  • 21 Jul 1886
    Mary O'Connor marries Peter RiordanKingwilliamstown RC. Their eight children were first cousins of the O'Keeffe children at Shanavoher.
  • 3 Apr 1883
    Marriage No. 1 — William O'Keeffe & Julia O'ConnorRC Chapel of Kingwilliamstown, Boherbuy District, Kanturk Union. William (bachelor, farmer, Ballydwane; father: John O'Keeffe) marries Julia (spinster, farmer's daughter, Glenalocha; father: Owen O'Connor). Witnesses: Owen Connor & John O'Keeffe — both fathers. M. McMahon P.P. Certificate No. 69.
    Source: Civil marriage certificate, Boherbuy District, Cork
  • 1885–94
    Six children born to William & Julia at ShanavoherJohn (1885), Eugene (1886), Julia (1888), Mary Anne/Sr. Mary Malachy (1890), Kate (1891), Bridget (1894). Oral history also records a twin William (died infant) and a Hannah (died aged 11) — to be confirmed in civil records.
  • 4 Jul 1898
    Julia O'Keeffe dies aged 35Shanavoher. Anarca Causa, 3 years certified. Six children aged 4–13 left motherless. William present at death.
    Source: Civil death certificate, Shanavoher 1898
  • 24 Jun 1899
    Mary O'Connor Riordan dies aged 30Glenalougha. Pneumonia, 2 weeks. Sister of Julia & Hannah O'Connor. Left seven children. Her daughter Hannah Riordan would survive the Titanic.
    Source: Civil death certificate, Glenalougha, 1899
  • 19 Feb 1901
    Marriage No. 2 — William O'Keeffe & Hannah O'ConnorRC Chapel of Kingwilliamstown. William (widower, farmer, Glounalougha) marries Johanna O'Connor (spinster, Julia's younger sister, Glounalougha). Witnesses: Eugene Lane & Julia Leary. Canon Denis J. O'Riordan P.P. Certificate No. 48. A papal dispensation was required under Catholic canon law. Canon O'Riordan was a great family friend and was later executor of William and Hannah's wills.
    Source: Civil marriage certificate, Boherbuy District, Cork
  • 31 Mar 1901
    1901 Census — O'Keeffe household, ShanavoherWilliam (head, 45, farmer), Hannah (wife, 30), John (16), Eugene (14), Julia (13), Mary (11), Katie (9), Bridget (7), servant Julia Fitzpatrick (19). William would be dead in 27 days.
  • 27 Apr 1901
    William O'Keeffe dies — 67 days after his weddingGlenalougha. Aged 47. Mitral Valve Disease, 3 months certified. Hannah present. She was widowed at ~30, almost certainly already pregnant with Ellen.
    Source: Civil death certificate, Glenalougha, registered 30 April 1901
  • 19 Dec 1901
    Ellen Mary (Eileen Maria) O'Keeffe bornGlenalocha. Hannah's only child, born 8 months after William's death. Known as a poultry farmer after studying at the Munster Institute, Cork.
  • 26 Apr 1902
    Julia O'Keeffe (daughter, 13) dies of tuberculosisGlenalougha. Phthisis (TB), 1 year 3 months; Diarrhoea, 3 months. No medical attendant. Informant: Hannah Keeffe, Step-mother. Entry No. 463. The first document where Hannah formally identifies as step-mother.
    Source: Civil death certificate, Boherbuy District 1902
  • 7 Mar 1911
    Mary Anne (Sr. Mary Malachy) O'Keeffe diesCallan, Co. Kilkenny. Aged 20, as a religious sister.
  • 11 Apr 1912
    Hannah Riordan boards the RMS Titanic at QueenstownHannah Riordan (first cousin of the O'Keeffe children) sailed 3rd class, rescued in Lifeboat 13. Married Jack Spollen 1936 in New York; died 29 Sep 1982 in the Bronx aged 91.
    Source: Encyclopedia Titanica; Fr Tom O'Keeffe family research 2026
  • 2 Apr 1911
    1911 Census — Hannah's household, ShanavoherHannah (head, 45, widow, farmer): 10 yrs married, 1 child born, 1 living. Present: Ellin (daughter, 9), Eugene (nephew, 24), Kate (niece, 19), Bridget (niece, 18).
  • 18 Apr 1926
    1926 Census — two O'Keeffe households at ShanavoherHousehold 1: Eugene (36) & Helena (29) at the main farm (10 acres) with servant Patrick Halloran. Household 2: Hannah (55, widow, farmer) with Katie (33), Bridget (32, in poor health), and Ellen (~24, dressmaker/poultry).
  • Jan 1940
    Eileen Maria O'Keeffe (aged 38) marries Maurice Murphy (aged ~67)RC Church of St. Peter & Paul, Cork City. Eileen Maria O'Keeffe (spinster, Lakevale, Ballydesmond) marries Maurice Murphy (widower, shopkeeper, Glashakinleen/Newmarket). Witnesses: Maurice S. Murphy & Hanna Carroll. Jeremiah Fehily P.P. Certificate No. 229. No children from this marriage.
    Source: Civil marriage certificate No. 229, No. 4 Urban District, Cork
  • 22 May 1940
    Hannah O'Keeffe diesLakevale. Aged ~69. Pneumonia, 2 weeks. Informant: Nolé O'Keeffe, Glashakinleen. She had devoted her entire adult life to the O'Keeffe family, and lived to see Ellen married just 4 months earlier.
    Source: Civil death certificate, Lakevale 1940
  • 17 Sep 1952
    John O'Keeffe dies in Oregon, USABend, Deschutes County, Oregon. Born 14 March 1885, Shanavoher. William & Julia's eldest son, who emigrated to the United States.
  • 9 Apr 1982
    Eileen Maria O'Keeffe Murphy diesGlashakinleen, Kiskeam. Aged 80. Hannah's only child. Widow of Maurice Murphy, shopkeeper. The Murphy family ran a shop in Kiskeam village (now Murphy Denis C.).
All Known Children of William O'Keeffe
By Julia O'Connor (1st wife, married 1883)
NameBornDiedNotes
John O'Keeffe14 Mar 188517 Sep 1952Emigrated to USA. Died Bend, Deschutes County, Oregon.
Eugene O'Keeffe22 Dec 188621 May 1966Married Helena Fitzgerald 1925. Took over Shanavoher farm. Father of William O'Keeffe; grandfather of Ger O'Keeffe.
Julia O'Keeffe188826 Apr 1902Died aged 13, TB, Glenalougha. Registered by Hannah as "step-mother."
Mary Anne O'Keeffe24 Apr 18907 Mar 1911Sr. Mary Malachy. Died aged 20 at Callan, Co. Kilkenny, as a religious sister.
Kate O'Keeffe11 Dec 189114 Feb 1974Died St. Patrick's Hospital, Kanturk. Never married. Lived with Hannah.
Bridget O'Keeffe189430 Apr 1971Went to England to train as nurse but did not finish. In poor health by 1926.
William O'Keeffe (twin)~1894InfantOral history: Twin of Bridget, died as infant. To be confirmed in civil records.
Hannah O'KeeffeUnknownAged 11Oral history: Died aged 11. To be confirmed in civil records.
By Hannah O'Connor (2nd wife, married 1901)
NameBornDiedNotes
Ellen/Eileen Maria O'Keeffe19 Dec 19019 Apr 1982Studied poultry at Munster Institute, Cork. Known as a poultry farmer. Married Maurice Murphy (widower, shopkeeper) January 1940, Cork City. No children. Died Glashakinleen, Kiskeam.
Lake Vale, Ballydesmond · c.1960

Eugene O'Keeffe (b.1886) and Helena Fitzgerald (b.1895) at the family home at Lake Vale, Ballydesmond, Co. Cork, with five of their children. The gas wall heater, wallpaper, and clothing style suggest this photograph was taken around 1960, when Eugene was approximately 74 and Helena approximately 65.

Left to Right — First Four
  • Nell O'Keeffe — b. 4 May 1930
  • Fr Tom O'Keeffe — b. 1932
  • Helena Fitzgerald O'Keeffe — b. 18 Oct 1895 · mother
  • Eugene O'Keeffe — b. 22 Dec 1886 · father
Left to Right — Last Three
  • Sheila O'Keeffe — b. 1 Sep 1926
  • Joan O'Keeffe — b. 6 Sep 1932
  • Willie O'Keeffe — b. 8 Mar 1928
Eugene died 21 May 1966 · Helena died 9 October 1967. This photograph was likely taken in the final years of their lives together at Lake Vale.
The Murphy Family of Glashakinleen — Ellen's Marriage
Maurice Murphy (Muiris Ua Murchadha) — Two Marriages
Born approximately 1873, Glashakinleen, Co. Cork · Died between 1940 and ~1955 (date to be confirmed)
Father
Andrew Murphy, farmer, Glashakinleen (deceased by 1940)
First Marriage (~1895–96)
Maria (surname unknown, b.~1874, National School Teacher). 8 children born, 7 surviving by 1911. Maria was alive in 1926 census aged ~59. She died between 1926 and 1940.
Second Marriage (January 1940)
Eileen Maria O'Keeffe (b.19 Dec 1901), at St. Peter & Paul, Cork City. No children. Maurice was ~67 at marriage; Eileen was 38.
Note
The Murphy family completed the 1926 census entirely in Irish — Maurice signing as "Muiris Ua Murchaídha" — a notable mark of the family's Irish language tradition in the Sliabh Luachra area.
William O'Keeffe & Alice O'Connor — Marriage 1965
William O'Keeffe
Born 8 Mar 1928, Kanturk · Died 7 Mar 2016, Lake Vale, Ballydesmond · Buried Ballydesmond
Parents
Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald, Lake Vale, Ballydesmond.
Alice O'Connor
Born 18 Jul 1931, Tralee, Co. Kerry · Died 7 Feb 2021, Ballydesmond, Cork
Parents
David "Dave Ned" O'Connor (1896–1970) & Kate O'Leary (1898–1982), Farrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry. (See Section 3.)
Siblings
Nora Mai O'Connor (c.1930–2019, married Patsy Fleming, Farrankeal; 13 children); Alice O'Connor (1931–2021, married William O'Keeffe, Ballydesmond); Jeremiah O'Connor (1933–2003, San Francisco); Peggy O'Connor (twin of Jeremiah, died as infant 3 Aug 1933).
St. Stephens Church · Castleisland · 25 February 1965
Wedding Photograph · 1965
William O'Keeffe
& Alice O'Connor
Married 25 February 1965 at St. Stephens Church, Castleisland, Co. Kerry. William was born in Kanturk in 1928, son of Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald of Lake Vale, Ballydesmond. Alice was born in Tralee in 1931, daughter of David O'Connor & Kate O'Leary of Farrankeal, Currow.
Parents of Joseph, Helena Mary, David Gerard (Ger), Thomas Anthony, Liam, and Dermot Brendan O'Keeffe.
Marriage — William O'Keeffe & Alice O'Connor · 25 February 1965
Location
St. Stephens Church, Castleisland, Co. Kerry
Groom
William O'Keeffe, born 8 March 1928, Kanturk. Son of Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald.
Bride
Alice O'Connor, born 18 July 1931, Tralee. Daughter of David "Dave Ned" O'Connor & Kate O'Leary, Farrankeal, Currow.
Children
Joseph (b.9 Jan 1966, Tralee), Helena Mary (b.14 Dec 1966, Cork), David Gerard (b.23 Jul 1969, Cork), Thomas Anthony (b.20 Jun 1970, Cork), Liam (b.8 Aug 1971, Cork), Dermot Brendan (b.3 Aug 1976, Cork).

1926 Census · Two O'Keeffe Households at Shanavoher · 18 April 1926
NameHouseholdRelationAgeStatusNotes
Eugene O'Keeffe1 (main farm)Head36Married ~2 yrsFarmer, own account, 10 acres. Father of William O'Keeffe; grandfather of Ger O'Keeffe.
Helena O'Keeffe1Wife29MarriedMother of William O'Keeffe; grandmother of Ger O'Keeffe. Born Gullane, Gneeveguilla.
Patrick Halloran1Servant34SingleFarm labourer.
Hannah Keeffe2 (second residence)Head55WidowFarmer, own account. Still head of own household.
Katie Keeffe2Niece33SingleDomestic (out of work). Julia's daughter.
Bridget Keeffe2Niece32SingleIn poor health. Julia's daughter.
Ellen Keeffe2Daughter~24SingleListed as dressmaker, own account — but known to family as a poultry farmer. Founded St. Rita's Pedigree Poultry Farm at Lake Vale in the early 1930s. Hannah's own daughter.
Marriage & Death Certificates — Part 1 (All Verified)
Marriage No. 1 — William O'Keeffe & Julia O'Connor · 3 April 1883
Location
RC Chapel of Kingwilliamstown, Boherbuy Registrar's District, Kanturk Union, Co. Cork
Groom
William O'Keeffe — full age, bachelor, farmer. Residence: Ballydwane. Father: John O'Keeffe, farmer.
Bride
Julia O'Connor — full age, spinster, farmer's daughter, Glenalocha. Father: Owen O'Connor, farmer.
Witnesses
Owen Connor (Julia's father) & John O'Keeffe (William's father)
Officiant
M. McMahon P.P. · Certificate No. 69, registered 3rd July 1883
Marriage No. 2 — William O'Keeffe & Johanna (Hannah) O'Connor · 19 February 1901
Location
RC Chapel of Kingwilliamstown, Boherbuy Registrar's District, Kanturk Union, Co. Cork
Groom
William O'Keeffe — full age, widower, farmer, Glounalougha. Father: John O'Keeffe, farmer.
Bride
Johanna O'Connor — full age, spinster, farmer's daughter, Glounalougha. Father: Owen O'Connor, farmer. (Julia's younger sister.) A papal dispensation was required under Catholic canon law.
Witnesses
Eugene Lane & Julia Leary
Officiant
Denis J. Canon O'Riordan P.P. · Certificate No. 48
Marriage — Eileen Maria O'Keeffe (aged 38) & Maurice Murphy (aged ~67) · January 1940
Location
RC Church of St. Peter & Paul, Cork City, No. 4 Urban Registrar's District
Groom
Maurice Murphy — full age, widower, shopkeeper, Glashakinleen/Newmarket. Father: Andrew Murphy (deceased), farmer.
Bride
Eileen Maria O'Keeffe — full age, spinster, Lakevale, Ballydesmond. Father: William O'Keeffe (deceased), farmer.
Witnesses
Maurice S. Murphy (likely Maurice's son from 1st marriage) & Hanna Carroll
Officiant
Jeremiah Fehily P.P. · Certificate No. 229. No children from this marriage.
Death — Julia O'Keeffe (née O'Connor) · 4 July 1898 · Shanavoher
Age & Condition
35 years. Married — wife of a farmer.
Cause
Anarca Causa, 3 years certified. Ill for three years while still bearing Kate (1891) and Bridget (1894).
Informant
William O'Keeffe, present at death, Shanavoher.
Death — Julia O'Keeffe (daughter, aged 13) · 26 April 1902 · Glenalougha
Cause
Phthisis (TB), 1 year 3 months; Diarrhoea, 3 months. No medical attendant.
Informant
Hannah Keeffe, Step-mother, present at death, Glenalougha. Entry No. 463, Boherbuy District.
Death — William Keeffe · 27 April 1901 · Glenalougha
Age & Condition
47 years. Married, farmer.
Cause
Mitral Valve Disease, 3 months certified. Illness from ~January 1901 — before the February wedding.
Informant
Hannah Keeffe, widow, Glenalougha. Registered 30 April 1901. James J. O'Riordan, Registrar.
Oral History Notes — Nellie O'Keeffe (Recorded 2026)
Additional Children of William & Julia — Not Yet in Records
Nell recalls a William — twin of Bridget — who died as an infant, and a Hannah who died aged 11. Both remain to be confirmed in civil death records, Newmarket/Kanturk district, c.1894–1902.
Bridget O'Keeffe — Nursing in England
Nell recalls Bridget went to England to train as a nurse but did not finish. She appears in the 1926 census aged 32, listed as "in poor health."
Canon O'Riordan — Family Friend and Executor of Wills
Canon Denis J. O'Riordan P.P., who officiated at William's 1901 marriage to Hannah, was a great family friend and later executor of both William's and Hannah's wills. This gives warm context to the 1901 marriage — it was not merely administrative but performed by someone the family trusted deeply.
Eileen O'Keeffe — St. Rita's Pedigree Poultry Farm, Lake Vale
Nellie O'Keeffe confirms Auntie Eileen attended the Munster Institute in Cork to study poultry and was known throughout her life as a poultry farmer. Original business documents survive from the 1930s that confirm her enterprise in remarkable detail.

St. Rita's Pedigree Poultry Farm, Lake Vale, Kingwilliamstown, Co. Cork — listed Miss O'Keeffe as Manageress on both a printed letterhead (dated "193...") and a promotional flyer. The farm specialised in White Leghorn hens and operated as a registered Pedigree Utility Department Poultry Station with all stock blood-tested for B.W.D. (Bacillary White Diarrhoea — standard certification of the period).

The farm achieved significant competition success: First Prize and First Class Certificate in the White Leghorn Section, Irish National Laying Test 1930–31, and Third Prize and First Class Certificate in the same section, 1931–32. Eggs were sold at 2/- per sitting (12 eggs), with day-old chicks to order. Printed by Duhallow Printing Works, Kanturk.

These are among the most vivid primary documents in the entire family collection — proof of a genuinely successful, prize-winning agricultural enterprise run by women in 1930s rural Cork.
Fr Thomas (Tom) O'Keeffe — Priest of the Dublin Diocese
Ordination Photograph · May 1960
Son of Eugene & Helena O'Keeffe · Lake Vale, Ballydesmond
Fr Thomas (Tom) O'Keeffe
Priest of the Archdiocese of Dublin · 1960–present
Fr Tom O'Keeffe is the youngest son of Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald of Lake Vale, Ballydesmond, Co. Cork — the family whose origins are traced in Sections 1 and 2 of this record. He is brother to Sheila, William (Willie), Nell, and Joan, and uncle to fourteen nieces and nephews. He was ordained in May 1960 and served for over sixty years as a priest of the Dublin Diocese.
Fr Tom presided at the baptisms, first communions, confirmations, marriages and funerals of his extended family across six decades — a living link between the O'Keeffe family of Ballydesmond and the generations that followed.
In His Own Words — Reminiscing at 90

"My upcoming 90th birthday has prompted me to share some of my memories as a priest in the Dublin Diocese for the past sixty-four years. I was born and reared in Lakevale, where my brother Willie and his wife Alice and family lived for many years. My sisters Nell and Joan are thankfully still alive to celebrate this milestone with me, as are my fourteen nieces and nephews, who are all very special to me."

"I've been very happy as a priest, where you get a great insight into human nature at times of people's greatest joys and deepest sorrows. Every day I thank God for my long and healthy life and for the many friendships that have enriched my life. It has been a great privilege to be able to minister to a wide range of people, young and old, urban and rural, in both the most disadvantaged and advantaged communities."

— Fr Thomas O'Keeffe, written on the occasion of his 90th birthday
Priestly Career — Appointments & Postings
  • May 1960
    Ordained to the PriesthoodOrdained for the Archdiocese of Dublin. Appointed Chaplain to the Little Sisters of the Assumption, Clondalkin, and teacher of religion at a vocational school in Cabra West. He made the eight-mile journey each morning by Heinkel scooter — then a novel mode of transport for a young priest, and forbidden to own a car by order of Archbishop McQuaid.
  • 1965
    Inner-City Ministry — Brugh Mhuire, Gardiner StreetAppointed to the Inner-City Boys' Club, around Gardiner Street, continuing to teach in a local vocational school. He described these two years as the hardest of his priestly life — running the youth club from 7pm until late each evening.
  • Late 1960s
    Curate, WhitehallFirst parish posting as curate at Whitehall, near Dublin Airport.
  • 1970s
    Chaplain, Holles Street Maternity Hospital — Westland Row ParishOne of the most formative postings of his career. Chaplain to what is now the National Maternity Hospital, Holles Street, Dublin 2. He witnessed births, supported unmarried mothers from the Dunboyne Mother and Baby Home, and gained a lifelong respect for the experience of motherhood. On his departure in 1980, the hospital's Executive Committee passed a resolution recording their admiration for what they called his "magnificent contribution to the welfare of the patients and staff."
    Source: Letter from National Maternity Hospital, 26 February 1980
  • Jan 1980
    Appointment to Haddington Road — then withdrawnThe Archbishop initially appointed Fr Tom to Haddington Road parish, but within days wrote to inform him the appointment would not proceed, pending completion of a Hospital Chaplaincy course. He remained at Westland Row.
    Source: Letters from Archbishop's House, 7 January 1980 & 16 January 1980
  • Jul 1980
    Curate, ShankillAppointed Curate in the parish of Shankill, Co. Dublin, with additional responsibility for pastoral care at St Gabriel's Hospital, Cabinteely.
    Source: Letter from Archbishop's House, 13 June 1980
  • Jun 1983
    Administrator, Larkhill/Whitehall ParishAppointed Administrator of the parish of Larkhill/Whitehall with sole responsibility for all parochial property and finance.
    Source: Letter from Archbishop's House, 10 June 1983
  • Mid-1980s
    Curate, FoxrockServed as Curate in Foxrock parish before his appointment as Parish Priest.
  • 25 May 1988
    Appointed Parish Priest, CabinteelyAppointed Parish Priest of Cabinteely by Archbishop Desmond Connell — the role he would hold for the remainder of his active ministry. The letter of appointment formally granted him all the Faculties of a Parish Priest and asked him to arrange the Ceremony of Induction within four weeks.
    Source: Letter from Archbishop Desmond Connell, Archbishop's House, 25 May 1988
  • 1986
    Mission Visit — The PhilippinesTravelled to the Philippines for a month with a Trócaire-hosted group of priests and lay people. The country was in political upheaval as President Marcos was displaced by Cory Aquino. The visit gave him a lasting perspective on the challenges of mission life and the relative ease of ministry in Ireland.
  • 2005–2006
    Parish Priest, Cabinteely — Curate changesFr Tom continued as PP at Cabinteely through several changes of curate arranged by Archbishop Diarmuid Martin, including Fr Michael O'Grady (2005), Fr Damian Farnon (2005–2006), and Fr Arthur O'Neill (2006).
    Source: Letters from Archbishop Diarmuid Martin, 4 July 2005 & 27 June 2006
  • Feb 2012
    Letter of Good Standing — Holiday in SpainArchbishop Diarmuid Martin wrote to the Bishop of Málaga confirming that Fr Tom was "a priest in good standing in the Archdiocese of Dublin" and requesting that he be facilitated in celebrating the sacraments — a small but telling document illustrating that even in retirement he remained an active priest.
    Source: Letter from Archbishop Martin to Bishop of Málaga, 23 February 2012
  • 1988–present
    Thirty-Six Years in Cabinteely — as PP and in retirementFr Tom spent the last thirty-six years of his ministry in Cabinteely Parish, retiring as Parish Priest but remaining active in the parish community. As he wrote at 90: "I've been very happy here and made great friends but my heart is always in Ballydesmond with my family and parish community."
Document — Appointment as Parish Priest · 25 May 1988
Archbishop's House · Dublin 9 · 25 May 1988
Archbishop's House · Dublin
Appointment as Parish Priest
Cabinteely · 25 May 1988
Signed by Archbishop Desmond Connell, D.D., this letter formally appointed Fr Thomas O'Keeffe as Parish Priest of Cabinteely — the post he had long served as Curate in Foxrock, and the parish where he would spend the remainder of his active ministry.

The appointment granted him all the Faculties of a Parish Priest and requested a Ceremony of Induction within four weeks. It also, as was canonical custom, asked him to submit his resignation upon reaching his 75th year.

Fr Tom remained at Cabinteely for thirty-six years — as Parish Priest and then in retirement. It is the longest posting of his priestly life.
Family Priest — Six Decades of Ministry to the O'Keeffe Family

Fr Tom O'Keeffe occupies a unique place in this family record. He is the son of Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald — the couple whose marriage in 1925 at Rathmore united the O'Keeffe and Fitzgerald lines documented in Sections 1 and 2. He is the brother of William O'Keeffe, who married Alice O'Connor in 1965, uniting all four Kerry families of Sections 1–4. And he is the uncle of David Gerard (Ger) O'Keeffe, in whose family this record has its origins.

From 1960 through the 2020s — spanning more than sixty years — Fr Tom was present at the baptisms, first communions, confirmations, marriages and funerals of his extended family. He is, in the deepest sense, the family's priest: a man who carried the O'Keeffe name from Ballydesmond into the life of the Church, and who ministered to the full range of human joy and sorrow for over six decades.

"It has been a great privilege to be able to minister to a wide range of people, young and old, urban and rural, in both the most disadvantaged and advantaged communities."
Kingwilliamstown / Ballydesmond — The Village
History of the Village
Ballydesmond (Irish: Baile Deasumhan), formerly Kingwilliamstown, is a rural village on the Blackwater River at the Cork-Kerry border. It was established in the 1830s as a model village and named after King William IV; previously known as Tooreenkeogh. In 1951 it was officially renamed Ballydesmond. It lies in the Sliabh Luachra area, famed for its traditional Irish music. From Samuel Lewis's Topographical Dictionary of Ireland (1837): the village was "recently erected by government, in the parish of NOHAVALDALY, barony of DUHALLOW, county of CORK." The O'Keefes are specifically mentioned as having occupied crown lands in the area since 1641. The Tureengarriffe ambush near Ballydesmond — where British army officers were killed by the local IRA flying column — occurred during the War of Independence. Nora Herlihy, a founder of the Irish League of Credit Unions, was from Ballydesmond.
Section 2
The Fitzgerald & Reen Family
Gullane, Gneeveguilla & Mount Infant, Knocknagree, Co. Cork · 1820s to 1967
Generation Tree · Section 2
Origins — Edmund Fitzgerald & Bridget Cronin / Michael Reen & Mary Bradley
Edmund (Ned) Fitzgerald & Bridget Cronin
Co. Kerry / Co. Cork border area
Thomas's Parents
Parents of Thomas E. Fitzgerald (b.1866, Gullane). Edmund Fitzgerald was a farmer; he is listed as deceased on Thomas's 1890 marriage certificate. The Fitzgerald family were based in the Gullane/Gneeveguilla area of Kerry.
Michael Reen & Mary Bradley
Tureenaroha, Knocknagree, Co. Cork
Ellen's Parents
Parents of Ellen Reen (b.abt 1857, Tureenaroha, Knocknagree). Ellen was baptised 28 May 1859 at Rathmore with Nohaval and Kiloummin, Kerry. Michael Reen was listed as living at the time of the 1890 marriage. Ellen died at Mount Infant, Knocknagree.
Thomas E. Fitzgerald & Ellen Reen — Helena's Parents
Thomas E. Fitzgerald & Ellen Reen
Thomas born 1866, Gullane, Gneeveguilla · Died 1940, Mount Infant · Ellen born abt 1857, Tureenaroha · Died Mount Infant
Marriage
18 February 1890, RC Chapel of Rathmore, Registrar's District of Coom, Union of Killarney, County Kerry. Thomas (bachelor, farmer, Mount Infant; father: Edmund Fitzgerald, deceased, farmer) married Ellen Keen/Reen (spinster, farmer's daughter, Tureenaroha; father: Michael Keen, living). Witnesses: Daniel Casey & Ellen Keen. Officiated by J.P. Barton C.C. Marriage No. 56, registered 30 March 1890.
Note on Residences
The marriage certificate (1890) shows Thomas residing at Mount Infant, yet Helena's birth certificate (1895) shows the family at Gullane. The oral history about the Runaway Bog explains this movement. All children up to ~1900 were born in Gullane; by 1911 the family was at Knocknagree/Mount Infant, Co. Cork.
Children (9 Known)
Bridget (b.18 Dec 1890), Mary (b.10 Jan 1892), Edmond (b.30 May 1893), Helena (b.18 Oct 1895, Gullane — mother of William O'Keeffe), Ellen (b.3 Oct 1896), Kate (b.24 Jan 1897), Hanora (b.3 May 1898, d.1963), Michael (b.2 Jun 1900), Margaret (b.abt 1904).
The Runaway Bog — How the Fitzgeralds came to Mount Infant

According to oral history recorded by Nellie O'Keeffe (2026), the Fitzgerald family originally lived at Gullane, Gneeveguilla — confirmed by the birth certificates of all children born up to c.1900. They moved to Mount Infant, Knocknagree following a local disaster known as the "Runaway Bog." Helena's uncle — the owner of Mount Infant — went out to help during the emergency, contracted a severe cold, and died as a result. Helena's father Thomas then inherited Mount Infant and the family moved there. The move likely occurred around 1900–1905.
Helena Fitzgerald — Key Records
Birth — Helena Fitzgerald · 18 October 1895 · Gulane, Gneeveguilla, Co. Kerry
Registration
Entry No. 53, District of Cooray (Coom), Union of Killarney, County Kerry. Registered 16 January 1896 by J. Sullivan, Registrar.
Father
Thomas E. Fitzgerald, farmer, Gulane.
Mother
Ellen Fitzgerald formerly Keen (Reen). Maiden name written as "Keen" on the certificate.
Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald — Marriage 26 November 1925
Eugene O'Keeffe
Born 22 Dec 1886, Shanavoher/Glenalougha · Died 21 May 1966, Lake Vale, Ballydesmond
Birth Cert
Father: William O'Keeffe, farmer, Shanavoher. Mother: Julia O'Keeffe formerly O'Connor. Registered 18 March 1887.
Burial
Ballydesmond Cemetery. Gravestone: "In Loving Memory Of EUGENE O'KEEFFE, Lake Vale. Died 21st May 1966. His Wife HELENA. Died 9th Oct. 1967. Their Daughter SHEILA. Died 7 April 1995."
Helena Fitzgerald
Born 18 Oct 1895, Gulane, Gneeveguilla, Kerry · Died 9 Oct 1967, Rathoat, Co. Meath
Parents
Thomas E. Fitzgerald (1866–1940) & Ellen Reen (b.abt 1857). Both buried at or near Mount Infant, Knocknagree.
In 1911
Aged 15, Knocknagree, Cork. Daughter in household, single. Buried at Ballydesmond Cemetery with Eugene and daughter Sheila.
Studio Portrait · c.1925
Photograph · c.1925
Eugene O'Keeffe
& Helena Fitzgerald
Married 26 November 1925 at the RC Church of Rathmore, Union of Killarney, Co. Kerry. Eugene was born in Glenalougha, Kingwilliamstown (Ballydesmond) in 1886, son of William O'Keeffe & Hannah O'Connor. Helena was born in Gullane, Gneeveguilla in 1895, daughter of Thomas Fitzgerald & Ellen Reen of Mount Infant, Knocknagree.
Witnesses: James O'Riordan & Kate Fitzgerald (Helena's sister).
Officiant: Canon Carmody, Rathmore.
Grandparents of Gerard (Ger) O'Keeffe.
Marriage — Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald · 26 November 1925 · No. 114
Location
RC Church of Rathmore, Registrar's District of Coom, Union of Killarney, County Kerry
Groom
Eugene O'Keeffe — full age, bachelor, farmer. Residence: Glenalougha, Kingwilliamstown. Father: William O'Keeffe, farmer.
Bride
Helena Fitzgerald — full age, spinster. Residence: Mount Infant, Knocknagree. Father: Thomas Fitzgerald, farmer.
Witnesses
James O'Riordan & Kate Fitzgerald (Helena's sister)
Officiant
J. Canon Carmody · Registrar's District of Coom, Kerry
Chronological Timeline — Fitzgerald & Reen Family
  • abt 1820s
    Edmund (Ned) Fitzgerald & Bridget Cronin — Thomas's grandparents' generationThe Fitzgerald family were based in the Gullane/Gneeveguilla area of Kerry. Edmund is listed as deceased by the time of Thomas's 1890 marriage certificate.
  • 1866
    Thomas E. Fitzgerald bornGullane, Gneeveguilla, Co. Kerry. Son of Edmund (Ned) Fitzgerald & Bridget Cronin, farmers.
  • abt 1857
    Ellen Reen bornTureenaroha, Knocknagree, Co. Cork. Daughter of Michael Reen & Mary Bradley. Baptised 28 May 1859 at Rathmore with Nohaval and Kiloummin, Kerry.
  • 18 Feb 1890
    Thomas Fitzgerald marries Ellen ReenRC Chapel of Rathmore, Registrar's District of Coom, Union of Killarney, Co. Kerry. Thomas (bachelor, farmer, Mount Infant; father: Edmund Fitzgerald, deceased) married Ellen Keen/Reen (spinster, farmer's daughter, Tureenaroha; father: Michael Keen, living). Witnesses: Daniel Casey & Ellen Keen. Officiated by J.P. Barton C.C. Marriage No. 56.
    Source: Civil marriage certificate, Registrar's District of Coom, Kerry
  • 1890–1904
    Nine children born to Thomas & Ellen at GullaneBridget (18 Dec 1890), Mary (10 Jan 1892), Edmond (30 May 1893), Helena (18 Oct 1895), Ellen (3 Oct 1896), Kate (24 Jan 1897), Hanora (3 May 1898), Michael (2 Jun 1900), Margaret (abt 1904). All children up to ~1900 born at Gullane, Gneeveguilla.
    Source: Civil birth certificates, Registrar's District of Coom, Kerry
  • 18 Oct 1895
    Helena Fitzgerald bornGulane, Gneeveguilla, Co. Kerry. Entry No. 53, District of Cooray (Coom), Union of Killarney. Registered 16 January 1896 by J. Sullivan, Registrar. Father: Thomas E. Fitzgerald, farmer. Mother: Ellen Fitzgerald formerly Keen (Reen).
    Source: Civil birth certificate, Boherbuy District 1895
  • c. 1900–1905
    The Runaway Bog — family moves from Gullane to Mount Infant, KnocknagreeHelena's uncle, the owner of Mount Infant, went out to help during a bog disaster, contracted a severe cold, and died. Thomas Fitzgerald inherited Mount Infant and the family relocated. The 1926 census confirms all family members recorded their birthplace as Gullane.
    Source: Oral history, Nellie O'Keeffe, 2026
  • 2 Apr 1911
    1911 Census — Fitzgerald household, KnocknagreeThomas and Ellen at Mount Infant, Knocknagree, Co. Cork. Helena aged 15, single, daughter in household. The family had moved from Gullane by this date.
    Source: Census of Ireland 1911, Knocknagree, Cork
  • 24 Nov 1925
    Helena Fitzgerald marries Eugene O'KeeffeRC Church of Rathmore, Registrar's District of Coom, Co. Kerry. Helena (full age, spinster, Mount Infant, Knocknagree; father: Thomas Fitzgerald, farmer) married Eugene O'Keeffe (full age, bachelor, farmer, Glenalougha, Kingwilliamstown; father: William O'Keeffe, farmer). Witnesses: James O'Riordan & Kate Fitzgerald (Helena's sister). Officiant: J. Canon Carmody.
    Source: Civil marriage certificate, Registrar's District of Coom, Kerry
  • 18 Apr 1926
    1926 Census — Eugene & Helena O'Keeffe at ShanavoherEugene (head, 36, married ~2 yrs, farmer, own account, 10 acres) and Helena (wife, 29) at the main Shanavoher farm, with servant Patrick Halloran. Both recorded as the parents of William O'Keeffe and grandparents of Ger O'Keeffe.
  • 1926–1934
    Five children born to Eugene & Helena at Lake Vale, BallydesmondSheila (1 Sep 1926), William (8 Mar 1928, Kanturk), Nellie (4 May 1930), Joan (6 Sep 1932), Tom/Fr. Tom (11 Nov 1934).
  • 1940
    Thomas E. Fitzgerald diesMount Infant, Knocknagree. Helena's father. Buried at or near Mount Infant, Knocknagree.
  • 19 Mar 1966
    Edmond Fitzgerald diesMallow, Co. Cork. Helena's brother, born 30 May 1893, Gullane. Aged 72.
  • 21 May 1966
    Eugene O'Keeffe diesLake Vale, Ballydesmond. Aged 79. Buried Ballydesmond Cemetery. Gravestone: "In Loving Memory Of EUGENE O'KEEFFE, Lake Vale. Died 21st May 1966."
    Source: Civil death certificate; gravestone inscription, Ballydesmond Cemetery
  • 9 Oct 1967
    Helena O'Keeffe (née Fitzgerald) diesRathoat, Co. Meath. Aged 71. Buried Ballydesmond Cemetery with Eugene and daughter Sheila. Her gravestone reads: "His Wife HELENA. Died 9th Oct. 1967."
    Source: Civil death certificate; gravestone inscription, Ballydesmond Cemetery
  • 1963
    Hanora Fitzgerald diesLisheen, Gneeveguilla. Helena's sister, born 3 May 1898, Gullane.
Children of Eugene O'Keeffe & Helena Fitzgerald

Sheila O'Keeffe1 Sep 19267 Apr 1995Lake Vale, Ballydesmond. Did not marry. Died St. James Hospital, Dublin.

Section 3
The O'Connor Family
Dromulton, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry · 1850s to 1970

This section documents the O'Connor family of Dromulton, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry — the paternal family of Alice O'Connor (born Tralee 1931, married William O'Keeffe 1965, mother of Ger O'Keeffe). The first recorded member of the Dromulton O'Connor family is John O'Connor, who married Hanora Browne from Mullin, Scartaglin. Their son Edmond O'Connor married Bridget Brosnan and is the grandfather of Ger O'Keeffe. It has been said that the O'Connor family lived on a farm 'below the church' beside Scartaglin village before settling at Dromulton. Family details in this section are sourced in part from the research of Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com (updated 01/06/2023).

Generation Tree · Section 3
Origins — John O'Connor & Hanora Browne (GL0, Dromulton)
John O'Connor & Hanora Browne
Dromulton, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry — first recorded generation at Dromulton
Background
John O'Connor married Hanora Browne from Mullin, Scartaglin. It has been said that the O'Connor family lived on a farm 'below the church' beside Scartaglin village before they settled at Dromulton. John and Hanora are the earliest confirmed members of the Dromulton O'Connor line.
Children (known)
Daniel (b.1852), David (b.1853), Edmond (b.1855), Elizabeth (b.1856), John (b.1858), Joanna (b.1859), Hanora (b.1861). Hanora O'Connor married John Batt Scanlon, Woodlands, Currans.
The Tragedy of Daniel O'Connor
Daniel O'Connor (b.1852) died young and tragically in an accident at Dromulton. He and a servant boy had spotted rabbits and both ran to get a gun kept in a ditch. In the rush, the gun discharged accidentally and Daniel was shot and died. The name Daniel thereafter 'died' at Dromulton — it was never given to another O'Connor child of the family. This oral account has been preserved by the family.
Source
Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com O'Connor Dromultan research (updated 01/06/2023).
Edmond John O'Connor & Bridget Brosnan (GL1, Dromulton)
Edmond (Neddie) John O'Connor & Bridget Brosnan
Edmond born abt 1855, Dromulton · Died 11 Jun 1923 · Bridget born abt 1857, Clune, Cordal · Died 27 Jul 1945
Parents
Edmond is the son of John O'Connor & Hanora Browne, Dromulton. He is baptised 5 April 1855, RC Castleisland. He married Bridget Brosnan and settled at Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Bridget's Parents
John Brosnan (1824–1887) & Johanna (Hannah) O'Connor (1837–1912). Bridget's mother was herself an O'Connor — a notable connection.
David "Dave Ned" O'Connor
Born 1 November 1894, Dromulton, Scartaglin · Died 13 January 1970, Farrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry
Son of Edmond & Bridget
Dave Ned married Kate 'Jim' O'Leary from Mount, Scartaglin on 4 February 1925. After marriage they lived on the O'Leary farm at Mount, Scartaglin — as confirmed by the 1926 census, which shows David assisting his father-in-law James O'Leary on the 87-acre farm. The family later moved to Farrankeal, Currow, Farranfore.
IRA Service
Scartaglin Company, Battalion I, Kerry II Brigade. Military Service Pension awarded £28 15s per annum from 1934. File 34-E-145, Irish Military Archives. See pension record below.
David (Dave Ned) O'Connor — IRA Veteran & Military Service Pensioner
David "Dave Ned" O'Connor
Born 1 November 1894, Dromulton, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry · Died 13 January 1970, Farrankeal, Carrow, Farranfore, Co. Kerry
Parents
Neddie (Edmond) O'Connor (abt 1855–1923) & Bridget Brosnan (abt 1857–1945), Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Marriage (Confirmed)
4 February 1925 — RC Church of Castleisland. Confirmed by both the civil marriage certificate (No. 402, Castleisland Registrar's District) and the Military Service Pension widow's application (File 34-E-145). Witnesses: Mich. Fos. O'Flynn & Maggie O'Leary (Kate's sister). Officiant: Archdeacon Casey.
Father of
Alice O'Connor (1931–2021), who married William O'Keeffe in 1965.
IRA Service & Pension
David served in Óglaigh na hÉireann, Scartaglin Company, Battalion I, Kerry II Brigade. He was awarded a Military Service Pension of £28 15s per annum from 1 October 1934 (File 34-E-145). Total certified service: 5¾ years. He was active during the War of Independence (1919–1921) and the Civil War (1922–23), served in an Active Service Unit (A.S.U.), and was interned in 1923. No criminal sentence on record.
RC Church · Castleisland · 4 February 1925
Wedding Photograph · 1925
David O'Connor
& Kate O'Leary
Married 4 February 1925 at the RC Church of Castleisland, Union of Tralee, Co. Kerry. David (full age, bachelor, farmer, Dromulton; father: Edmond O'Connor, farmer) married Catherine J. O'Leary (full age, spinster, farmer's daughter, Scartaglin; father: James O'Leary, farmer). Officiated by Archdeacon Casey.
Witnesses: Mich. Fos. O'Flynn & Maggie O'Leary (Kate's sister).
Marriage No. 402 · Castleisland Registrar's District · Certificate No. 05305228
Parents of Alice O'Connor (b.1931, Tralee), wife of William O'Keeffe and mother of Gerard (Ger) O'Keeffe.
Marriage Certificate — David O'Connor & Catherine (Kate) J. O'Leary · 4 February 1925
Location
RC Church of Castleisland, Union of Tralee, Registrar's District of Castleisland, Co. Kerry. Marriage No. 402. Certificate No. 05305228.
Groom
David O'Connor — full age, bachelor, farmer. Residence: Dromulton. Father: Edmond O'Connor, farmer.
Bride
Catherine J. O'Leary — full age, spinster, farmer's daughter. Residence: Scartaglin. Father: James O'Leary, farmer.
Witnesses
Mich. Fos. O'Flynn & Maggie O'Leary (Kate's sister, daughter of James O'Leary & Hannah Scanlan).
Officiant
Archdeacon Casey, RC Church of Castleisland.
Military Service Pension — David O'Connor · Irish Military Archives, File 34-E-145
Archive Ref.
File 34-E-145 / 34SP/5743 / A/C No. 91-1084. Military Service (1916–1923) Pension Project. Irish Military Archives.
Unit
Scartaglin Company, Battalion I, Kerry II Brigade. Irish Volunteers (1916–1919) → Óglaigh na hÉireann (1919–1923).
Service Periods
No active service credited for 1916–1919 (Irish Volunteers period). Active service credited from April 1919 during the War of Independence and Civil War. Period 1 April–11 July 1921: entire period credited. Civil War service July 1922–March 1923 in Active Service Unit (A.S.U.), Kerry II Brigade: entire period. Followed by internment April–September 1923.
Pension Awarded
£28 15s 0d per annum from 1 October 1934. Grade of rank: E. Service Certificate issued 26 July 1935; Award Certificate issued 23 August 1935. David signed his acknowledgement from Mount, Scartaglin on 10 August 1935. Witnessed by Edmund Murphy, Head Teacher, Scartaglin National School.
Widow's Pension
After David's death (13 January 1970), Kate O'Connor applied for a widow's allowance, assisted by Mr. Timothy O'Connor TD, Killorglin, Co. Kerry. Kate was granted £56 14s 4d per annum from 1 October 1971 under the Army Pensions Act, 1971. Quarterly warrants of £29.46 were paid to Kate at Farrankeal until her death on 2 March 1982.
Historical Note — David O'Connor's IRA Service
The War of Independence & Civil War in Kerry — David O'Connor's Role
David O'Connor served with the Scartaglin Company, Battalion I, Kerry II Brigade of Óglaigh na hÉireann (the IRA) during the Irish War of Independence (1919–1921) and the Civil War (1922–1923). Kerry was one of the most intensely contested counties during both conflicts. The Kerry II Brigade covered the Castleisland/Scartaglin/Farranfore area — the heartland of the O'Connor and O'Leary families.

His service record shows active participation from 1919 onwards, with the most intense involvement in 1921 (the War of Independence final phase) and 1922–23 (the Civil War). During the Civil War he served in an Active Service Unit (A.S.U.) — a mobile guerrilla flying column — and was subsequently interned (April–September 1923). The Department of Justice confirmed "no record of sentence," meaning he was held as a political internee rather than a convicted prisoner.

He applied for his pension under the Military Service Pensions Acts in 1935, signing his declaration at Mount, Scartaglin on 26 August 1935 — witnessed by Edmund Murphy, Head Teacher of Scartaglin National School. His pension of £28 15s per annum was modest; Grade E was the lowest grade of the pension scale, reflecting active participation without officer rank.
Jeremiah O'Connor — The Last Son Leaves Mount, Scartaglin

David (Dave Ned) O'Connor and Kate O'Leary had three children born at Mount, Scartaglin in the late 1920s and early 1930s. Their only son was Jeremiah O'Connor, born 9 July 1933. In approximately 1964 — the date recalled by his daughter Kathleen, though not confirmed — Jeremiah emigrated to San Francisco, California. He was the only son of an only son, and his departure from Mount effectively closed the O'Connor chapter at Scartaglin. He never returned to farm the land. The farm passed from the family. It was a moment that would have been keenly felt — a son of the house of Dave Ned O'Connor, IRA veteran, grandson of the Dromulton O'Connors, leaving Kerry for California.

Jeremiah O'Connor & Anna O'Connor
Jeremiah: b. 9 July 1933, Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry · d. 7 April 2003, San Francisco · Anna: b. 19 June 1932 · d. 20 January 2003, San Francisco
Parents
David (Dave Ned) O'Connor & Kate O'Leary, Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Jeremiah was their only son. His sisters were Nora Mai O'Connor (b.c.1930, married Patsy Fleming, Farrankeal; died 13 June 2019), Alice O'Connor (b.1931, married William O'Keeffe, Ballydesmond), and Peggy O'Connor (b.9 Jul 1933, died 3 Aug 1933, infant, twin of Jeremiah).
Emigration
Emigrated to San Francisco, California in approximately 1964 (recalled by his daughter Kathleen Hanley; not independently confirmed). Sponsored into the USA by members of the Brosnan family — part of the extensive Kerry emigrant network in the Bay Area. His first work was as a construction labourer, including major projects such as the Bank of America building in downtown San Francisco. His departure as an only son meant the O'Connor presence at Mount, Scartaglin came to an end.
Marriage
Married Anna in San Francisco on 14 May 1966. Both died within three months of each other in early 2003 — Jeremiah on 7 April, Anna on 20 January. They are buried in San Francisco.
Children — born San Francisco
Kathleen O'Connor — b. 10 April 1968. Married Patrick Hanley. Children: Ryan Connor Hanley (b.1995) and Emily Anne Hanley (b.1997).

Eileen O'Connor — b. 16 October 1969.

David O'Connor — b. 30 March 1973. Children: Aidan James O'Connor (b.2002) and Jack Michael O'Connor (b.2002), twins.

Anna Maria O'Connor — b. 30 January 1977, d. 26 June 2019. RIP. Mother of Ronan Anthony Wiles (b. 28 May 2012).
Note
The living generation — David O'Connor and his sons Aidan and Jack, and Anna Maria's son Ronan — is documented in Section 7, Living Generations.
San Francisco · May 2002
Photograph · May 2002
Jeremiah O'Connor
& Anna O'Connor
Jeremiah O'Connor (b. 9 July 1933, Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry) and Anna O'Connor (b. 19 June 1932), photographed at a family celebration in San Francisco in May 2002 — less than a year before both died in early 2003. Jeremiah is the son of David (Dave Ned) O'Connor & Kate O'Leary of Mount, Scartaglin.
Jeremiah died 7 April 2003 · Anna died 20 January 2003
Both are buried in San Francisco.
Oral History — Kathleen Hanley (née O'Connor) on Her Father's Arrival in San Francisco
Kathleen O'Connor Hanley, Jeremiah's eldest daughter, recorded the following recollection about her father's early years in San Francisco:

"I'm pretty sure it was 1964 but I'm not positive. I know one of the Brosnan's sponsored him into USA and they helped him get construction jobs. His first job was working a jackhammer and he would have us crying laughing describing how it shook his false teeth up. He worked big jobs like the Bank of America building downtown and smaller projects with the Brosnans."

The Brosnan connection is notable — the Brosnan family were a significant part of the Kerry emigrant network in San Francisco, sponsoring new arrivals and providing work through their construction contacts. Jeremiah's grandmother on his father's side was Bridget Brosnan of Clune, Cordal, Co. Kerry — it is possible these San Francisco Brosnans were part of the same extended Kerry family, though this has not been confirmed.
Source: Oral history, Kathleen O'Connor Hanley, 2026
Chronological Timeline — O'Connor Family, Part 3
  • pre-1850
    O'Connor family at Scartaglin — 'Below the Church'It has been said that the O'Connor family lived on a farm below the church beside Scartaglin village before settling at Dromulton. The first recorded member of the Dromulton line is John O'Connor, who married Hanora Browne from Mullin, Scartaglin.
    Source: Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com O'Connor Dromultan (01/06/2023)
  • 1852–1861
    Children born to John O'Connor & Hanora Browne, DromultonDaniel (b.1852), David (b.1853), Edmond (b.1855), Elizabeth (b.1856), John (b.1858), Joanna (b.1859), Hanora (b.1861). Hanora later married John Batt Scanlon, Woodlands, Currans.
    Source: Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com
  • c.1852–55
    Tragic death of Daniel O'Connor, DromultonDaniel O'Connor (b.1852) died young in an accident. He and a servant boy had spotted rabbits and both ran to retrieve a gun kept in a ditch. In the rush the gun discharged accidentally and Daniel was shot and died. The name Daniel thereafter 'died' at Dromulton — never given to another O'Connor child of the family.
    Source: Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com O'Connor Dromultan (oral history preserved)
  • 5 Apr 1855
    Edmond (Neddie) John O'Connor bornDromulton, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Son of John O'Connor & Hanora Browne. Baptised 5 April 1855, RC Castleisland.
  • abt 1857
    Bridget Brosnan bornClune, Cordal, Co. Kerry. Daughter of John Brosnan (1824–1887) & Johanna (Hannah) O'Connor (1837–1912). Bridget's mother was herself an O'Connor — a notable connection.
  • ~1880s
    Edmond O'Connor marries Bridget BrosnanDate and location to be confirmed from civil records. They settled at Dromulton, Scartaglin.
  • 1888–1901
    Children born to Edmond & Bridget at Dromulton, ScartaglinJohn (1888), Patrick (1891), Hannah (1893), David/Dave Ned (1 Nov 1894), Eddie (1897), Michael (1900), Alice (1901), and others including John, Nora, Annie O'Connor.
    Source: Civil birth records; Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com
  • 11 Jun 1923
    Edmond (Neddie) O'Connor diesDromulton, Scartaglin. Aged approximately 68. Father of David O'Connor.
  • 4 Feb 1925
    David O'Connor marries Kate 'Jim' O'LearyRC Church of Castleisland, Union of Tralee, Co. Kerry. David (full age, bachelor, farmer, Dromulton; father: Edmond O'Connor, farmer) married Catherine J. O'Leary (full age, spinster, farmer's daughter, Scartaglin; father: James O'Leary, farmer). Witnesses: Mich. Fos. O'Flynn & Maggie O'Leary (Kate's sister). Officiant: Archdeacon Casey. Marriage No. 402. After marriage, David and Kate lived on the O'Leary farm at Mount, Scartaglin — confirmed by the 1926 census.
    Source: Civil marriage certificate No. 402, Castleisland Registrar's District, Co. Kerry; Irish Military Archives, File 34-E-145
  • c.1930
    Nora Mai O'Connor bornMount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Eldest surviving daughter of David (Dave Ned) O'Connor & Kate O'Leary. She appears as a young child in the family photograph taken at Mount circa 1930, seated with her mother Kate Jim. She later married Patsy Fleming and settled at Farrankeal, Currow — the same townland where Dave Ned and Kate spent their later years. Mother of 13 children, 40 grandchildren and 6 great-grandchildren. Died 13 June 2019 in her 90th year.
    Source: Death notice, Nora Mai Fleming (née O'Connor), June 2019
  • 18 Jul 1931
    Alice O'Connor bornTralee, Co. Kerry. Daughter of David O'Connor & Kate O'Leary. Later married William O'Keeffe in 1965. Mother of Ger O'Keeffe.
  • 27 Jul 1945
    Bridget Brosnan O'Connor diesConnie Kerins, Scartaglin. Aged approximately 88. Mother of David O'Connor.
  • 9 Jul 1933
    Jeremiah O'Connor bornMount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Only son of David (Dave Ned) O'Connor & Kate O'Leary. Named after Kate's brother Commandant Jer O'Leary, killed at Castleisland in 1923.
  • 3 Aug 1933
    Peggy O'Connor born and diesMount, Scartaglin. Twin sister of Jeremiah, died as an infant. Jeremiah was the only son of Dave Ned & Kate. His sisters Nora Mai and Alice remained in Kerry; Jeremiah emigrated to San Francisco.
    Source: Civil registration records, Co. Kerry 1933
  • c.1964
    Jeremiah O'Connor emigrates to San FranciscoJeremiah O'Connor (b.1933, Mount, Scartaglin), only son of Dave Ned O'Connor & Kate O'Leary, emigrated to San Francisco, California in approximately 1964. Sponsored into the USA by members of the Brosnan family — part of the established Kerry emigrant network in the Bay Area. His first work was as a construction labourer, operating a jackhammer on major projects including the Bank of America building in downtown San Francisco. His departure as the only son effectively closed the O'Connor chapter at Mount, Scartaglin — the farm passed from the family. He would never return to live in Kerry.
    Source: Oral history, Kathleen O'Connor Hanley, 2026
  • 14 May 1966
    Jeremiah O'Connor marries Anna, San FranciscoMarried in San Francisco on 14 May 1966. Anna was born 19 June 1932. They settled in San Francisco and raised four children.
  • 10 Apr 1968
    Kathleen O'Connor bornSan Francisco. Eldest child of Jeremiah & Anna O'Connor. Later married Patrick Hanley. Mother of Ryan Connor Hanley (b.1995) and Emily Anne Hanley (b.1997).
  • 16 Oct 1969
    Eileen O'Connor bornSan Francisco. Second child of Jeremiah & Anna O'Connor.
  • 13 Jan 1970
    David (Dave Ned) O'Connor diesFarrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry. Father of Jeremiah O'Connor and grandfather of the San Francisco branch. Aged 75.
    Source: Military Service Pension file 34-E-145
  • 30 Mar 1973
    David O'Connor bornSan Francisco. Third child of Jeremiah & Anna O'Connor. Later father of twins Aidan James and Jack Michael O'Connor (b.2002).
  • 30 Jan 1977
    Anna Maria O'Connor bornSan Francisco. Youngest child of Jeremiah & Anna O'Connor. Mother of Ronan Anthony Wiles (b.2012).
  • 1982
    Kate O'Leary O'Connor diesMount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Wife of Dave Ned O'Connor. Aged approximately 84. She had tended the memory of her brother Commandant Jer O'Leary — killed at Castleisland in 1923 — every year until her death, taking out his scarf on the anniversary of his death, 29 May.
    Source: Family records; oral history
  • 20 Jan 2003
    Anna O'Connor diesSan Francisco. Wife of Jeremiah O'Connor. Born 19 June 1932. Aged 70.
  • 7 Apr 2003
    Jeremiah O'Connor diesSan Francisco. Son of Dave Ned O'Connor & Kate O'Leary. Born 9 July 1933, Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Aged 69. He and Anna died within three months of each other. Both are buried in San Francisco.
  • 13 Jun 2019
    Nora Mai Fleming (née O'Connor) diesFarrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry. Died peacefully at home in her 90th year, surrounded by her loving family. Beloved wife of the late Patsy Fleming. Survived by 13 children (Davy, Mary, Sheila, Jud, Anne, Pius, Sean, Seamus, Kay, Paul, Noreen, Con and Kieran), 40 grandchildren and 6 great-grandchildren, and her sister Alice O'Keeffe of Ballydesmond.
    Source: Death notice, RIP.ie, June 2019
  • 26 Jun 2019
    Anna Maria O'Connor diesSan Francisco. Youngest child of Jeremiah & Anna O'Connor. Born 30 January 1977. Aged 42. Mother of Ronan Anthony Wiles (b.2012). RIP.

Daniel O'Connor1852Died young in a tragic shooting accident at Dromulton. The name Daniel was never used again in the family.
David O'Connor1853Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Edmond (Neddie) O'Connor1855Baptised 5 April 1855, RC Castleisland. Married Bridget Brosnan. Father of Dave Ned O'Connor.
Elizabeth O'Connor1856Dromulton, Scartaglin.
John O'Connor1858Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Joanna O'Connor1859Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Hanora O'Connor1861Married John Batt Scanlon, Woodlands, Currans.
Source: Séamus Fleming, fkancestry.com O'Connor Dromultan research (01/06/2023)
Children of Edmond (Neddie) O'Connor & Bridget Brosnan — GL1, Dromulton, Scartaglin
NameBornDiedNotes
John O'Connorabt 1888—Co. Kerry.
Patrick O'Connorabt 189110 Jan 1937Died Dromulton, Scartaglin, aged ~46.
Hannah O'Connorabt 1893—Co. Kerry.
David O'Connor1 Nov 189413 Jan 1970Married Kate O'Leary, 4 February 1925. Father of Alice O'Connor. IRA veteran, Military Service Pensioner. Lived at Mount, Scartaglin after marriage, later Farrankeal, Currow.
Eddie O'Connor9 Jul 18971985Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Michael O'Connor19001984Dromulton, Scartaglin.
Alice O'Connorabt 1901—Co. Kerry.
John, Nora, Annie O'Connor———
Section 4
The O'Leary Family
Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry · 1746 to 1982

This section documents the O'Leary family of Mount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry — the maternal family of Alice O'Connor. The O'Learys have farmed at Mount, Scartaglin since at least the mid-18th century, with William O'Leary (b.1746) recorded on a headstone at Kilsarcon Cemetery and his son Michael O'Leary established as the sole O'Leary land tenant at Mount in the Griffith's Valuation of 1853. James (Jim) O'Leary and Hannah Scanlan ran the 87-acre farm at Mount through the late 19th and early 20th century. Their daughter Kate O'Leary married David O'Connor (~1925), bringing the O'Connor and O'Leary families together. James and Hannah are the great-grandparents of Ger O'Keeffe on the maternal side.

Generation Tree · Section 4
Deep Origins — William O'Leary (b.1746) to Michael O'Leary (GL0)
The O'Leary Ancestry at Mount, Scartaglin — Four Generations

The earliest confirmed O'Leary ancestor is William O'Leary (b.1746, d.1 May 1798, aged 52), whose headstone stands in the O'Leary plot at Kilsarcon Cemetery. The inscription reads: "Here Lieth the body of Wm Leary who departed This life may the 1st 1798 Aged 52 years. This Tomb was Erected by his three [sons]." The remainder is buried beneath concrete. William had three sons; Michael was one.

His son Michael O'Leary (b.c.1776, d.c.1872, aged ~96) married Margaret Hickey of Scartaglin and is the only O'Leary land tenant recorded at Mount (then marked as Slievebricken on maps) in the Griffith's Valuation of 1853. Michael and Margaret reared at least four children at Mount: Jeremiah, William, Bridget (who married Cornelius Kerin) and Mary. A precious rent book for the Mount farm survives for the period 1852–1876, beginning at £17 per half-year in 1852 and rising to £28 by 1876 — with allowances for liming and drainage of the heavy boggy land. Michael's name signs off rent payments until 1870–71, after which Jeremiah's name takes over.

Michael shared the farm at Mount between his two farming sons, Humphrey and James-Jim. Five sons — William, Cornelius, Jeremiah, Thade and Maurice — emigrated to the USA.
Origins — Jeremiah O'Leary (GL1) & Ellen Kerins
Jeremiah (Michael) O'Leary & Ellen Kerins
Jeremiah born 1817/21, died 28 Feb 1902, Scartaglin, aged ~83 · Ellen Kerins born 1830, Knockrour · died 17 Dec 1903, Mount, Scartaglin
Son of Michael O'Leary & Margaret Hickey
Jeremiah married Ellen Kerins from Knockrour, c.1852/1853, and farmed at Mount, Scartaglin. Together they reared 11 living children — 9 boys and 2 girls at Mount Scartaglin. Jeremiah is recorded as Head of Family at Mount in the 1901 census, aged 84. He had passed by the 1911 census. The farm at Mount was divided between his two farming sons Humphrey and James-Jim.
Children (GL2)
Michael (b.1853, settled Gurtloss), Humphrey (married Johanna Fitzgerald, farmed the 'Free' half of Mount), James/Jim (b.11 Dec 1860, married Hannah Scanlan 1894 — see below), William (emigrated USA ~1885–90), Cornelius (b.1874, emigrated USA, NYPD — see note), Patrick (b.1865, d.1930), Jeremiah, Maurice/Thade, Margaret, Nora (both married with children).
James (Jim) O'Leary & Hannah Scanlan
James born 11 Dec 1860, Mount, Scartaglin · Died 15 Feb 1941, Glenlaran, Cordal · Buried Castleisland
Hannah born 1859, Knockapreohane, Brosna, Co. Kerry · Died 24 Nov 1934, Mount, Scartaglin · Buried Kilbannivane Cemetery, Castleisland
Farm
87 statute acres at Mount, Scartaglin — a substantial holding inherited from Jeremiah O'Leary's division of the farm. Listed as employer on the 1926 census. Irish & English speaker.
Marriage
8 February 1894, Brosna, Co. Kerry. James was 33, Hannah was approximately 35. Hannah was daughter of Edmond Scanlan & Catherine 'Kate' Horan of Knockaprechane (Knockafreaghaun), Brosna, Co. Kerry. Her brother Timothy Scanlon is recorded as Head of Family at Enockafreaghaun in the 1901 census.
Burial
Hannah is buried at Kilbannivane Cemetery alongside her only son Jeremiah O'Leary, who was shot on 29 May 1923 at Castleisland. James is buried at Castleisland.
Cornelius O'Leary — Fourth Deputy Police Commissioner of New York City

Cornelius O'Leary (b.1874, Mount, Scartaglin, d.1947) was the brother of James (Jim) O'Leary and therefore Kate O'Leary's uncle. He emigrated to the United States around 1885–1890 and was appointed to the New York City Police Department as a uniformed policeman on 8 June 1901. He rose to the position of Fourth Deputy Police Commissioner of New York City — a remarkable achievement for a farmer's son from Scartaglin.

In the family photograph taken at Mount, Scartaglin circa 1930, Cornelius is pictured on a visit home — standing beside his brother Jim. The photograph captures life at Mount at that time: washed clothes hung out to dry on the whitethorn bushes on the haggard, a stile leading up behind the house. It was among the last gatherings of the family there; Hannah Scanlan died just a few years later on 24 November 1934.
Family Photograph — Mount, Scartaglin, c.1930
Mount, Scartaglin · Co. Kerry · c.1930

Cornelius O'Leary (b.1874, d.1947) returns home on a visit to Mount, Scartaglin from New York, where he is Fourth Deputy Police Commissioner of the city. The photograph captures life at Mount as it was — washed clothes hung out to dry on the whitethorn bushes on The Haggard up behind, a stile leading up to the haggard. Jim Leary's wife Hannah Scanlon died soon afterwards on 24 November 1934 and rests at Kilbannivane Cemetery alongside her only son Jeremiah O'Leary, who was shot on 29 May 1923 at Castleisland.

Back Row · Left to Right
  • Hannah Scanlon — Jim Leary's wife, holding Baby Alice her granddaughter, daughter of Kate Jim O'Leary
  • Dave Ned O'Connor — husband of Kate Jim O'Leary
  • Cornelius O'Leary — on a visit home from New York
  • James 'Jim' O'Leary — Con's brother, by his side
  • Nell / Ellie O'Leary O'Loughlin — who married Michael O'Loughlin, Cordal
Front Row · Seated
  • Kate Jim O'Leary O'Connor — daughter of Jim & Hannah, with her daughter Nora Mai O'Connor Fleming (who married Patsy Fleming, Farrankeal)
  • Local helper / assistant — holding Nell's daughter Peggie O'Loughlin
Baby Alice — held by Hannah in this photograph — later became Alice O'Connor O'Keeffe of Lake Vale, Ballydesmond, wife of William O'Keeffe and mother of Ger O'Keeffe. Photograph courtesy of Nora Mai Fleming (c.1930–2019), daughter of Kate Jim O'Leary & Dave Ned O'Connor, granddaughter of Jim & Hannah O'Leary.
Chronological Timeline — Part 4
  • b. 1746
    William O'Leary bornMount, Scartaglin area, Co. Kerry. The earliest confirmed O'Leary ancestor. Died 1 May 1798, aged 52. His headstone stands at the O'Leary plot in Kilsarcon Cemetery, erected by his three sons. Michael was one.
    Source: Kilsarcon Cemetery headstone; fkancestry.com O'Leary research
  • b. c.1776
    Michael O'Leary bornSon of William O'Leary. Married Margaret Hickey of Scartaglin. Farmed at Mount (then recorded as Slievebricken on maps). The sole O'Leary land tenant at Mount in the Griffith's Valuation of 1853. Died c.1872, aged ~96. Reared at least four children: Jeremiah, William, Bridget and Mary. Divided the farm between sons Humphrey and James-Jim.
    Source: Griffith's Valuation 1853; rent book 1852–1876; fkancestry.com O'Leary research
  • 1852
    Rent book begins — Mount, Scartaglin farmA surviving rent book records the O'Leary farm at Mount from 1852 to 1876. Rent starts at £17 per half-year (1852), rising to £28 by 1876, with allowances for liming and drainage of the heavy boggy land. Michael O'Leary's name signs rent payments until 1870–71, after which Jeremiah's name takes over.
    Source: O'Leary rent book 1852–1876, held in family collection
  • c.1852
    Jeremiah O'Leary marries Ellen KerinsJeremiah (b.1817/21, son of Michael & Margaret Hickey) married Ellen Kerins from Knockrour, c.1852/1853. Together they reared 11 living children — 9 boys and 2 girls — at Mount, Scartaglin.
  • 11 Dec 1860
    James (Jim) O'Leary bornMount, Scartaglin, Co. Kerry. Son of Jeremiah O'Leary & Ellen Kerins. Baptism sponsors: Maurice Kerin & Hanora Brosnan.
  • 1859
    Hannah Scanlan bornKnockapreohane (Knockafreaghaun), Brosna, Co. Kerry. Daughter of Edmond Scanlan & Catherine 'Kate' Horan, Knockaprechane, Brosna. Her brother Timothy Scanlon farmed at Enockafreaghaun; the Scanlon family also held a household at Knockeencreen, Gneeves.
  • c.1885–90
    Cornelius O'Leary emigrates to the United StatesCornelius (b.1874, brother of James-Jim O'Leary) emigrates to the USA. He was appointed to the New York City Police Department on 8 June 1901, rising eventually to Fourth Deputy Police Commissioner of New York City. He returned to visit Mount, Scartaglin circa 1930, captured in the family photograph.
    Source: fkancestry.com O'Leary research; family photograph c.1930
  • 8 Feb 1894
    James O'Leary marries Hannah ScanlanBrosna, Co. Kerry. James was 33, Hannah was approximately 35. They settled at the old homestead at Mount, Scartaglin where James farmed 87 acres inherited from his father Jeremiah.
  • 1895–1903
    Six children born to James & Hannah O'Leary at Mount, ScartaglinJeremiah (1895, the only son — see below), Nell (21 Apr 1896), Ellie (1897), Kate (1 Jul 1898), Margaret/Maggie (1900), Nora/Sister Kevin (abt 1903).
  • 31 Mar 1901
    1901 Census — O'Leary household, Mount/ScartaglinJeremiah O'Leary (head, 84, farmer), Ellen O'Leary (wife, 71), James O'Leary (son, 40, farm labourer), Hanna O'Leary/Scanlan (daughter-in-law, 36, general housekeeper), grandchildren: Jamie (6), Ellie (5), Katie (3), Magie (1). Also servants Patrick Connor (27) & Ellie Hannan (17). Jeremiah would be dead the following year.
    Source: Census of Ireland 1901, Mount/Scartaglin, Kerry
  • 28 Feb 1902
    Jeremiah (Michael) O'Leary diesScartaglin, Kerry. Aged ~83–85. James O'Leary's father. After his death, James became head of the Mount, Scartaglin household.
  • 17 Dec 1903
    Ellen Kerins O'Leary diesMount, Scartaglin. James O'Leary's mother, aged ~73.
  • 29 May 1923
    Commandant Jeremiah (Jer) O'Leary killed — Hartnetts Hotel, CastleislandJames & Hannah's only son, born at Mount, Scartaglin 1894, died aged 29. IRA Commandant and O/C of the 7th Battalion, Kerry No. 1 Brigade. Following the Civil War ceasefire, Jer surrendered at Cordal and was taken to Hartnetts Hotel, Castleisland. The following morning he was shot in the back in the barracks yard. His death left Kate O'Leary as the only surviving child at home — with profound consequences for inheritance of the 87-acre farm. Hannah Scanlan is buried at Kilbannivane Cemetery alongside her son Jer. See full account below.
    Source: Dying for the Cause; Irish Military Archives, 7th Battalion, Kerry No. 1 Brigade, RO 95
  • c.1930
    Family photograph taken at Mount, ScartaglinA remarkable photograph captures the O'Leary household at Mount circa 1930, with Cornelius O'Leary home on a visit from New York, standing beside his brother Jim. Hannah Scanlan holds Baby Alice — her granddaughter, daughter of Kate & Dave Ned O'Connor. Alice would later become the mother of Ger O'Keeffe.
    Source: Photograph courtesy of Nora Mai Fleming, granddaughter; fkancestry.com
  • 18 Apr 1926
    1926 Census — James O'Leary household, ScartaglinJames O'Leary (head, 65, married, farmer, 87 acres, employer), Hannah O'Leary (wife, 64), David O'Connor (son-in-law, 30, assisting on O'Leary farm), Kate O'Connor née O'Leary (daughter, 28, 1 yr married, 2 children), Maggie O'Leary (daughter, 26, single, assisting on farm).
    Source: Census of the Irish Free State 1926, Scartaglin, Kerry (Schedule 64)
  • 18 Jul 1931
    Alice O'Connor bornTralee, Co. Kerry. Daughter of David O'Connor & Kate O'Leary. Later married William O'Keeffe in 1965. Mother of Ger O'Keeffe.
  • 24 Nov 1934
    Hannah Scanlan O'Leary diesMount, Scartaglin. Aged approximately 75. Buried Kilbannivane Cemetery, Castleisland, alongside her son Commandant Jeremiah O'Leary. Kate O'Leary's mother.
  • 15 Feb 1941
    James (Jim) O'Leary diesGlenlaran, Cordal. Aged approximately 80. Kate O'Leary's father. Buried Castleisland, Co. Kerry.
  • 13 Jan 1970
    David (Dave Ned) O'Connor diesFarrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry. Father of Alice O'Connor.
  • 2 Mar 1982
    Kate O'Leary O'Connor diesFarrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry. Aged ~84. Mother of Alice O'Connor.

Kate O'Leary appears here as "Katie O'Leary, Granddaughter, aged 3." Signed by Jeremiah O'Leary, Head of Family. Enumerator: Constable Patrick Tohy.
1926 Census · James O'Leary Household · 18 April 1926 · Scartaglin, Kerry — Schedule 64
NameRelationAgeStatusBirthplaceNotes

Hannah O'Leary (née Scanlan)Wife64MarriedKnocknapreigan, Co. KerryHome duties. 32 yrs married, 5 children born.

This census confirms David O'Connor & Kate O'Leary married approximately 1925 and were living with Kate's parents. David was helping farm 87 acres at Scartaglin. Signed by James O'Leary.
Children of James O'Leary & Hannah Scanlan
NameBornDiedNotes
Jeremiah (Jer) O'Leary189429 May 1923IRA Commandant, O/C 7th Battalion, Kerry No. 1 Brigade. Only son. Killed by Free State soldiers at Hartnetts Hotel, Castleisland, aged 29. Buried Republican Plot, Kilbannivane Cemetery, alongside his mother Hannah. Celtic cross erected at Scartaglin, unveiled May 1946. His death meant Kate inherited the family farm. See full account below.
Nell O'Leary21 Apr 1896—Mount, Scartaglin. Married Michael O'Loughlin, Cordal. Appears in family photograph c.1930.
Ellie O'Learyabt 1897—Mount, Scartaglin.
Kate O'Leary1 Jul 18982 Mar 1982Mount, Scartaglin. Married David O'Connor 4 February 1925. Maternal grandmother of Ger O'Keeffe. On 29 May each year until her death, Kate took out the scarf her brother Jer wore when he was killed — and shed a tear.
Margaret (Maggie) O'Leary19001985Mount, Scartaglin. Still single and at home in 1926, assisting on farm.
Nora O'Leary (Sr. Kevin)abt 19031981Mount, Scartaglin. Became a religious sister.
Hannah Scanlan's Family — Census Records, Knockafreaghaun & Knockeencreen, Brosna, Co. Kerry

Hannah Scanlan (wife of James O'Leary) was born in 1859 at Knockapreohane (Knockafreaghaun), Brosna, Co. Kerry. Her parents were Edmond Scanlon & Catherine 'Kate' Horan of Knockaprechane. The Scanlon family are recorded across several households in the Gneeves area of Brosna in both 1901 and 1911. Her brother Timothy Scanlon is recorded as Head of Family at Enockafreaghaun in 1901.

1901 Census · Scanlon Household · House 16, Enockafreaghaun, Gneeves, Kerry — Hannah's Sibling Household
NameAgeSexRelation to HeadOccupationLiteracyMarital Status
Timothy Scanlon30MaleHead of FamilyFarmerRead and writeNot Married
Kate Scanlon80FemaleAunt—Read and writeNot Married
Edmond Scanlon25MaleBrotherFarmer's SonRead and writeNot Married
Mary Scanlon34FemaleSisterFarmer's DaughterRead and writeNot Married
Margaret Murphy18FemaleServantGeneral Domestic ServantRead and writeNot Married
Timothy (30) is likely Hannah Scanlan's younger brother. Kate Scanlon (80, listed as Aunt) was born c.1821 — possibly Hannah's great-aunt or an older generation connection. Hannah herself had married James O'Leary in 1894 and was living at Mount, Scartaglin by 1901.

Timothy Scanlon (now 49, married) is the same Timothy recorded as Head at Enockafreaghaun in 1901, aged 30 and unmarried. By 1911 he had married and had four living children.

John Scanlon (46) and his wife Hanora had been married 20 years by 1911, with 7 children born and 5 living. This is the same household as House 4, Knockeencreen in 1901. The younger children Johanna, Lily and Dannie are Irish and English speakers — a sign of the active Irish language tradition in the Brosna area.

David D. Horan (64, widower) — the Horan family of Knockafreaghaun are directly connected to the Scanlons: Hannah Scanlan's mother was Catherine 'Kate' Horan of Knockaprechane. David Horan is likely a relative of Hannah Scanlan's mother. Edmond Scanlon (30, farm servant here) matches the Edmond from the 1901 Enockafreaghaun household (aged 25 in 1901).
Children of David O'Connor & Kate O'Leary
NameBornDiedNotes
Nora Mai O'Connorc.193013 Jun 2019Born Mount, Scartaglin. Married Patsy Fleming, Farrankeal, Currow, Co. Kerry. Died peacefully at home in her 90th year, 13 June 2019. Mother of 13 children: Davy, Mary, Sheila, Jud, Anne, Pius, Sean, Seamus, Kay, Paul, Noreen, Con and Kieran. 40 grandchildren, 6 great-grandchildren. She appears as a young child in the Mount Scartaglin family photograph c.1930, seated with her mother Kate Jim.
